judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations and are comparable; Report 1 is marginally more thorough on the migration action and shadow-sink removal, while Report 2 adds extra findings (missing Dockerfile, no id-token permission).

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3` `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` ... Should be **omitted entirely** ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` — an allow-list of a test account with superadmin privileges ... Must be **removed entirely** from prod. ... Fix: remove `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` entirely from prod ... `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN` ... Should be removed unless someone can show a live consumer.

report_2.evidence: `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` ... Should be `"false"`. ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` ... This must not exist in prod at all — should be removed/empty ... the sink must be cleared (empty string) if shadow scoring is genuinely meant to be off in prod

report_1.evidence: that URL is baked into the compiled JS bundle at build time, not read from the runtime environment. ... **the exact same image/digest is deployed to both environments** ... the production container will serve a frontend bundle whose API calls are hardcoded to `https://staging-api.example.com` ... project documentation's "rebuild per environment; don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap

report_2.evidence: VITE_`-prefixed vars are inlined into the bundle at build time ... Both `deploy-staging` ... and `deploy-prod` ... then deploy **the same already-built image digest** ... the production launch would ship a frontend bundle whose API base URL is hardcoded to `https://staging-api.example.com` — the production UI would talk to the staging backend.

report_1.evidence: **No database migration step anywhere in the workflow.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` asserts "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in `deploy.yml` runs Alembic or any migration command ... don't assume the pipeline succeeded means the schema is current ... don't trust it without a live check.

report_2.evidence: The runbook (`docs/prod-runbook.md:6`) asserts "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but no migration step is visible in `deploy.yml`, and the only migration files present ... are stubs whose `upgrade()` bodies are just `pass` — there's no actual schema to verify migrations against

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` sets the **same** condition string, `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` ... This must be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production` before apply, per project documentation's explicit rule that "a stricter environment needs its own scoped trust condition, never an inherited or OR'd-together condition shared with a looser environment."

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` has the *same unedited* condition — `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` — even though the `deploy-prod` job runs under `environment: production` ... This must be changed to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"` before applying.
